description: You are an aerospace engineering instructor preparing a GPredict demonstration that shows how NASA's Deep Space Network provides global satellite coverage from three ground complexes. Configure GPredict with three DSN ground stations: Goldstone (35.4267 N, 116.8900 W, 1031m), Madrid (40.4314 N, 3.9527 W, 830m), and Canberra (35.4014 S, 148.9817 E, 680m). Create three tracking modules named DSN_Goldstone, DSN_Madrid, and DSN_Canberra, each assigned to its respective ground station and tracking ISS (ZARYA) NORAD 25544, CSS (TIANHE) NORAD 48274, NOAA 19 NORAD 33591, and AO-7 NORAD 7530. Ensure each module uses its specific ground station for pass predictions and that the application displays UTC time. Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'.
